c1.speaker: narrator
c1.line: Warren Holt stands before an audience of pigeons, reading from handwritten pages. The pigeons appear attentive. One has opinions.

c2.speaker: warren
c2.line: '—and so the waiting ends where it began: together.' Hm. Too much? The pigeons liked the second draft better, but pigeons are sentimental.

c3.speaker: gary
c3.line: Eight years of guardianship ends tonight. Any regrets?

c4.speaker: warren
c4.line: Regrets are for men who kept the wrong things warm, Mr. Gibbons. I kept a chair warm. Tonight I give it back with the seat still good. Come early; Poppy has assigned the press a row, and I'd hate to watch her improvise.
